title: San Rafael prepares site for authorized homeless camp
excerpt_text: The northern portion of Mahon Creek Path will offer 47 tent spaces, restrooms, security and access to social services.

selected_paragraphs.text: "San Rafael is confident that the temporary sanctioned camping area is a critical next step to help the homeless folks on Mahon Path move from encampments into permanent supportive housing," San Rafael Mayor Kate Colin said. "The current situation isn't healthy or safe for the people in the encampments and there continues to be a negative impact on the surrounding area."
Mel Burnette, the city's housing and homelessness analyst, said everyone living there complied with moving to the southern portion of Mahon Creek Path, allowing city contractors to begin work.

Through the program, the county will oversee outreach and case management services through one full-time outreach worker and three full-time housing case management workers. The sanctioned camp program includes contracting with Defense Block Security, the nonprofit Downtown Streets Team and other case management and service providers.

"This is a good first step," said Mark Shotwell, chief executive officer of the Ritter Center, a nonprofit that provides services to homeless residents and people in need. "It's not the long-term solution, but it will provide the kind of support people need, and the security and services with minimal impact on the neighborhood."

In another court case, a federal judge dismissed a lawsuit on Sept. 4 that blocked the city from evicting a homeless man from his campsite at the Menzies parking lot and Falkirk Cultural Center. Mark Rivera, who filed the suit, was evicted Sept. 9. Camping is prohibited there because the site is within 250 feet of a school.
